title: Warfarin/INR result review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports documenting clinician-performed benefit-risk discussion, adherence and control review, renal and bleeding context, annual reassessment, and documented decisions without generating a medicine choice or dose.
red_flags.rationale: NG196 supports clinician review of anticoagulation quality, bleeding or stroke risk context, patient preferences, and an entered anticoagulation decision when warfarin is used for AF; it does not interpret an INR or change a dose.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting clinician-performed benefit-risk discussion, adherence and control review, renal and bleeding context, annual reassessment, and documented decisions without generating a medicine choice or dose.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1-1.6.17 and 1.11.1-1.11.2 - anticoagulation discussion and review

medication.text: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.
medication.rationale: NG5 supports structured clinician review of medicine safety, monitoring, concerns, and documented decisions without producing a dose or adjustment.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

sources_used.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation

additions.text: Supports documenting clinician-performed benefit-risk discussion, adherence and control review, renal and bleeding context, annual reassessment, and documented decisions without generating a medicine choice or dose.
additions.rationale: NG196 supports clinician review of anticoagulation quality, bleeding or stroke risk context, patient preferences, and an entered anticoagulation decision when warfarin is used for AF; it does not interpret an INR or change a dose.
additions.evidence_extract: Supports documenting clinician-performed benefit-risk discussion, adherence and control review, renal and bleeding context, annual reassessment, and documented decisions without generating a medicine choice or dose.

source_limitations: Warfarin indication, target INR, actual result and date, trend, missed doses, diet, alcohol, interactions, illness, bleeding or thrombosis symptoms, clinician interpretation, dose decision, repeat interval, escalation, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
